UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores profile information for users interacting with the AI coaching app, complementing an external authentication system by holding non-sensitive user data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

CoachingSession.title: CoachingSession
CoachingSession.description: Represents a single coaching session interaction between a user and the AI coach.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CoachingSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who participated in this session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CoachingSession)

startTime.description: The exact timestamp when the coaching session began.

endTime.description: The exact timestamp when the coaching session ended.

durationMinutes.description: The total duration of the session in minutes.

status.description: The current or final status of the coaching session (e.g., 'started', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'failed').

summary.description: A brief, AI-generated summary or outcome of the coaching session.

SessionMessage.title: SessionMessage
SessionMessage.description: Stores individual messages or turns exchanged during a CoachingSession, capturing both user input and AI responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SessionMessage entity.

sessionId.description: Reference to the CoachingSession this message belongs to. (Relationship: CoachingSession 1:N SessionMessage)

sender.description: Identifies the source of the message (e.g., 'user', 'ai').

contentType.description: The type of content in the message (e.g., 'text', 'audio').

textContent.description: The textual content of the message, if applicable.

audioUrl.description: URL to the recorded audio content of the message, if applicable.

timestamp.description: The exact timestamp when the message was sent or recorded.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is strictly restricted to the user whose `uid` matches the `{userId}` path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual coaching sessions for a specific user. Includes the `userId` field for authorization independence, allowing direct ownership checks. Access is restricted to the user whose `uid` matches the `{userId}` path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this session, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the coaching session.

definition.description: Stores messages exchanged within a specific coaching session. CRITICAL: Includes a denormalized `userId` field (copied from the parent user) to ensure Authorization Independence without requiring `get()` calls to parent collections. Access is restricted to the user whose `uid` matches the denormalized `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent session, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent coaching session.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the session message.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is based on a hierarchical, user-centric model that prioritizes data segregation and authorization independence. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to the authenticated user's `uid`.

1.  **UserProfile:** Stored at `/users/{userId}`, where the document ID `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. This provides direct path-based ownership, making security rules simple: `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`.

2.  **CoachingSession:** Stored as a subcollection at `/users/{userId}/coachingSessions/{sessionId}`. Each `CoachingSession` document includes a `userId` field which holds the ID of the owning user. This field is consistent with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, reinforcing ownership and supporting authorization checks without requiring `get()` operations on the parent `UserProfile` document. This adheres to Authorization Independence.

3.  **SessionMessage:** Stored as a subcollection at `/users/{userId}/coachingSessions/{sessionId}/sessionMessages/{messageId}`. To ensure **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)** and simplify security rules, each `SessionMessage` document **must include a denormalized `userId` field**, copied from its grandparent `UserProfile` (or parent `CoachingSession`). This allows security rules at the `sessionMessages` level to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` directly, completely avoiding expensive and non-atomic `get()` calls to parent documents like `coachingSessions` or `users`. This is fundamental for robust and debuggable security.

**QAPs (Queryable Access Patterns) Support:**
This structure naturally enables secure `list` operations. Because all user-specific data is strictly partitioned under `/users/{userId}`, any `list` query from the client for `coachingSessions` or `sessionMessages` will automatically be scoped by the `request.auth.uid` in the path. Security rules can enforce that the `{userId}` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, ensuring that users can only query their own data. The denormalized `userId` field in `CoachingSession` and `SessionMessage` documents further allows for efficient, server-side filtering and robust rule enforcement for all read and write operations, directly supporting QAPs by ensuring rules are not acting as filters but as access enforcers on well-defined data scopes.
